just constantly folded in to our personalities and gifts and certainly our circumstances.<|22.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I'm thinking about Dr. Lise Barber, who's a member of this parish.<|6.96|> <|6.96|> Her specialty is palliative care.<|10.24|> <|10.24|> She's a wonderful person, but I know for a fact she's a great doctor, not because we've<|14.00|> <|14.00|> ever for Kaiser.<|24.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And there's this constant theme that I hear about being with Dr. Barber from some of our families.<|7.30|> <|7.44|> I've heard it <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> You know, I'm going to miss you, but I'm going to be okay.<|4.02|> <|5.26|> I'm really going to be okay.<|6.54|> <|7.70|> And he died shortly thereafter.<|10.08|> <|11.20|> I also want to take this opportunity to remind all of us<|14.24|> <|14.24|> that even when a person is not conscious,<|17.20|> <|17.74|> and Dr. Barber would be the first to tell us this,<|20.24|> <|20.28|> even when a person is not conscious,<|21.74|> <|22.46|> they can still really need to hear something.<|26.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Now, Dr. Barber is not a magician. It doesn't always work.<|3.42|> <|4.42|> But sometimes it does.<|6.02|> <|6.10|> So I think about how in Dr. Barber's life, love is paired with healing, a gift for healing.<|13.28|> <|13.52|> Healing in this case, not being cured, but healing in this case, a deeper form of healing,<|20.74|> <|20.96|> which is acceptance and release.<|24.90|> <|25.66|> Okay.<|26.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I think about another member of our parish, Fran Trujillo. Fran Trujillo is a former head of school.<|6.10|> <|7.04|> If you're ever looking for somebody to, you need to hire someone or to serve on your committee or<|12.10|> <|12.10|> get something done, trust me, hire a former head of school or principal. And Fran just really knows<|18.32|> <|18.32|> what she's doing. She's got just an organizational mind and is brilliant and knows how to herd cats and all of that. And<|25.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We've got across the street, actually a few properties across the street, or a couple.<|8.16|> <|8.70|> We've got the Kimberly Apartments on the corner, and we've got the St. Francis Apartments at Cathedral Square,<|13.80|> <|13.98|> and we've got the fenced-in area, which some of you walk across, which has the labyrinth,<|18.44|> <|18.44|> and we've got encumbered parking with the Argonauts.<|21.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> All of that's over there.<|2.20|> <|2.20|> And you'll hear more and more about that in the years to come,<|4.26|> <|4.32|> about how this stewardship, a task force,<|7.30|> <|7.36|> is trying to be better stewards of all of these properties.<|10.54|> <|10.72|> But at the St. Francis Apartments,<|12.30|> <|13.44|> which has been open for a few years,<|14.64|> <|14.72|> there are about 50 people who live there<|16.90|> <|16.90|> who were formerly experiencing homelessness<|20.28|> <|20.28|> on the streets of Denver and moved dramatically in.<|23.08|> <|23.54|> It's overseen by the St. Francis Center.<|25.22|> <|25.44|> And Fran...<|26.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> from the very beginning has been in charge of the relationship between our community and the community at the apartments.<|5.30|> <|5.78|> She does a great job of it in part because she always reminds us and me that the real goal is to do something with us.<|15.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> the tenants at the apartments, not necessarily for.<|4.00|> <|4.00|> So every now and then people have a great idea, like I've got a closet I need to clear out,<|8.00|> <|8.00|> can we bring the coats over and give them to people, or I've got some extra food,<|12.00|> <|12.00|> can we bring it over? And all those are good ideas.<|15.00|> <|15.00|> But Fran often reminds us that the best idea is not to do something for another group,<|21.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But all of your lives are a commentary,<|23.10|> <|24.20|> a wonderful commentary on this gospel reading about love.<|27.30|> <|27.30|> Love.<|27.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I question to us,<|8.70|> <|8.86|> and I'll paraphrase it.<|10.00|> <|10.32|> If you only love people who love you back, what good is that?<|14.92|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.28|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> If you only love people who love you back, what good is that?<|4.34|> <|5.46|> Jesus' own life is the most profound <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.32|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But the cross is simply in the background of this teaching, not in minutes to say what the collect of the day says in about 10 seconds.<|19.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And that opening prayer, Kallik, puts it like this.<|3.10|> <|3.20|> It give thanks to God for acts of love and justice<|7.70|> <|7.70|> in the church and in the world,<|10.08|> <|10.42|> whenever they occur and wherever they occur.<|12.40|> <|13.62|> And we pray continually when we need more of God's love<|20.26|> <|20.26|> that God will, with God's help, deliver even more.<|24.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And of course, we pray that petition each and every day of our lives.<|5.56|><|endoftext|>
